decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio and material bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the localized temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
Higher conductivity materials may have cost or weight implications not modeled here.
